title: Network and JSON parsing failures escape the route's JSON error contract
reasoning: The handler consistently returns JSON for its own validation failures, but the external `fetch` and subsequent `tokenRes.json()` call are unguarded. A Spotify outage, timeout, DNS failure, or malformed upstream response will throw and bypass the explicit JSON responses, so clients can receive a framework-generated 500 instead of the documented JSON error shape for this route.
reproduction: Simulate `fetch` rejecting or `tokenRes.json()` throwing; the exception will propagate out of `GET` because there is no `try/catch` around lines 25-42.
recommendation: Wrap the token exchange and JSON parsing in `try/catch`, map transport/parsing failures to a stable JSON error response, and consider passing through upstream status details in a sanitized way for easier debugging.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No tests were included for upstream failure paths, so nothing verifies that this route preserves its JSON contract when Spotify is unavailable or returns invalid payloads.
suggestedRegressionTest: Mock `global.fetch` to reject and separately to return a non-JSON body, then assert the route responds with a JSON error object and deterministic status code in both cases.
